title: Jacobaea tenuifolia
taxonomy: Jacobaea tenuifolia is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, commonly known as the slender hawk's beard or Jacobaea. It belongs to the genus Jacobaea, which is a group of plants in the Asteraceae family. Jacobaea tenuifolia is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. Within the Asteraceae family, it is classified under the subfamily Carduoideae and tribe Cardueae. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family.
uses: Jacobaea tenuifolia, also known as narrow-leaved hawk's beard, is a plant with potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues.
